utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi kenneth how are you the medical assistant told me that you had some knee pain

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i was getting ready for the holiday weekend and i was out on my boat skiing and i i did a jump and kinda twisted when i landed and my knee has been hurting me ever since

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so that was about five days ago then

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah yeah that was last weekend

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: last weekend okay now which knee is it

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it's my right knee

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: your right knee okay and i know that it sounds like you were on a motor boat as you were you know water skiing but did you hear anything pop or feel anything pop when it happened

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no it just felt like something stretched and then it swelled up some afterwards

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and were you i assume that you were were you able to get out of the water by yourself or did you need some assistance

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i was able to get out but it was very sore to climb up and you know any kind of squatting or bending is really pretty sore

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah okay and have you ever injured this knee before

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no that was the first time

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: that was the first time okay where do you have a boat what lake do you have it on lake

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: lake martin

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay nice and so you're a frequent water skier

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i try to go every weekend when we can

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: that's nice very very good are you looking forward to spring are you a baseball fan are you excited by opening day

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no i'm not a baseball fan but i love spring and chicken time of year launds of outdoor activities nice nice i'm i'm not really a baseball fan either but my husband makes me watch the the meds all the time and they lose all the time so he is always upset so so anyway

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah right so how about your high blood pressure how are you doing with that are you taking your blood pressure medication like i asked

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm taking it everyday and i check my blood pressure at home and it's been about one twenty five over eighty most of the days

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: nice okay so i believe we have you on lisinopril about twenty milligrams a day any side effects from that that you're noticing

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: not that i know of it's been a good medicine for me and i do n't have any trouble with it

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay great alright and since you had this knee pain any numbing or tingling in your foot at all

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no just the swelling and the pain

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and what have you taken for the pain

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i i took some aleve twice a day some over the counter aleve twice a day

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: putting a cold pack on it

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and has that helped at all

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah that's helped a fair amount it's still pretty sore though

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright well let's see have you ever had any surgeries before let me just think any surgeries i do n't see any in your in your record here

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i had my tonsils out

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay you had your tonsils out okay alright well let me go ahead i wan na do a just a quick physical exam i'm gon na go ahead and be calling out some of my clinical exam findings and i'll let you know what that means when i'm done okay so looking at your vital signs here in the office it does look like you're doing a really good job managing your blood pressure your blood pressure is up is about one twenty over seventy seven today here in the office and that's with you probably in a little bit of pain so that's good and on your neck exam i do n't appreciate any lymphadenopathy on your heart exam your heart is a nice regular rate and rhythm i do n't appreciate any murmur on your lung exam your lungs are clear to auscultation bilaterally on your musculoskeletal exam on your right knee i do appreciate some ecchymosis some edema there is an effusion present does it hurt when i press

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah that's sore

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay there is pain to palpation of the right medial knee there is i'm just gon na bend your knee in all sorts of directions here does that hurt

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah do n't do that anymore

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright the patient has decreased range of motion there is a negative varus and valgus test there is a negative lachman sign there is a palpable dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulse there is otherwise no lower extremity edema so what does that what does that mean kenneth so that so that means that you essentially i agree with you you had quite quite an injury to your knee and you do have a little bit of fluid in your knee there and just some inflammation which i think we need to talk about okay so i wan na go ahead and just talk a little bit about you know my assessment and my plan for you so for your first problem of your right knee pain i do believe you have what we call a a medial collateral ligament strain you know i wan na go ahead and just order an x-ray of your right knee just to make sure that we're not missing any broken bones which i do n't think we are but what's good about this particular injury is that people typically heal quite well from this and they typically do n't need surgery we can just go ahead and refer you to physical therapy to to strengthen those muscles around your knee so that you do n't have another injury and i wan na go ahead and just prescribe meloxicam fifteen milligrams once a day and that will help take down the swelling and help with some of the pain and you only have to take it once a day and it wo n't really cause any upset stomach or anything like that do you have any questions about that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah if i start that medicine today am i going to be able to ski tomorrow because we got a a big weekend plan

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i would say i would like you to rest your knee i i think that my concern is that if you go skiing again and you fall that you might injure your knee even more so i think you'll recover from this injury right now but i do n't want you to injure it anymore so i would rest it for a little while at least until we have the results of the x-ray and that type of thing okay for your second problem of your hypertension i wan na just go ahead and continue on lisinopril twenty milligrams a day i wan na just go ahead and order an a lipid panel just to make sure everything is okay from that standpoint and then i'm just looking through your health record and it looks like you're due for a tetanus shot so we'll go ahead and just give you a a tetanus shot for a from a health maintenance perspective any other questions

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i think so sounds like a good plan to me

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay sounds good alright so i'll see you later we'll get those ordered and i'll be in touch okay take care bye

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: thank you

detail_list.summary: The doctor initiates the visit to discuss the patient's knee pain.
The patient reports twisting his knee while water skiing five days ago.
The patient identifies the right knee as the site of pain.
The doctor asks if the patient heard or felt a pop during the injury.
The patient denies hearing a pop but reports a stretching sensation followed by swelling.
The patient reports soreness with squatting and bending.
The doctor asks if the patient has a history of injury to this knee.
The patient denies any prior injury to the right knee.
The patient keeps his boat on Lake Martin.
The patient is a frequent water skier.
Hypertension
The doctor asks if the patient is taking his blood pressure medication.
The patient reports taking his medication daily and monitoring blood pressure at home with readings around 125/80.
The patient is taking lisinopril 20 mg daily.
The doctor asks if the patient is experiencing any side effects from the medication.
The patient denies any side effects from the lisinopril.
The doctor asks if the patient has any numbness or tingling in his foot.
The patient denies numbness or tingling in the foot.
The doctor asks what the patient has taken for the pain.
The patient has been taking over-the-counter Aleve twice a day.
The patient has been using cold packs on the knee.
The doctor asks about the patient's surgical history.
The patient reports a history of tonsillectomy.

The doctor performs a physical exam, noting blood pressure 120/77, supple neck, clear lungs, regular heart rate/rhythm, and right knee findings including ecchymosis, effusion, pain to palpation, decreased range of motion, negative varus/valgus/Lachman tests, and palpable pulses.
The doctor assesses the patient with a medial collateral ligament strain.
The doctor orders an x-ray of the right knee.
The doctor provides a referral to physical therapy.
The doctor prescribes meloxicam 15 mg once a day.
The patient asks if he can go skiing tomorrow if he starts the medication today.
The doctor advises the patient to rest the knee until x-ray results are received.
The doctor confirms the patient should continue lisinopril 20 mg daily.
The doctor orders a lipid panel.
The doctor administers a tetanus vaccine in the office.
